blocks.content: Grafana Cloud queries your data where it already lives, so you don't have to move it. In this guide you'll connect an existing data source and confirm you can query it.

You need a Grafana Cloud user with the **Admin** role and a reachable data source. Don't have one yet? You can use the built-in **TestData** data source to generate sample time series and still complete this guide.
You'll open Connections and add a data source.
Open the main menu and click **Connections**.
Search for your data source, then click its tile. If you don't have one, search for **TestData** and select it.
Click **Add new data source** to open the configuration page.
Enter the connection details for your data source, such as a fully qualified host name or URL. Grafana Cloud connects over the public network, so use a public address, not localhost or a private IP.
If your data source requires credentials, enter them now. Use an account with least-privilege, read-only access.
Click **Save & test** to confirm Grafana Cloud can reach your data source.
You've connected and tested a data source. Grafana Cloud can now query it in place.
Now confirm you can query your data.
Go to **Explore**, select your new data source, and run a query.
You've queried your own data in Grafana Cloud.
As you connect more data, keep an eye on cost. Usage scales with the data you query and store. Refer to [Cost management and billing](/docs/grafana-cloud/cost-management-and-billing/).
Want to send new telemetry instead of querying existing data? Reopen **Get started with Grafana Cloud** and choose **Send your first metrics**.

---

### More to explore (optional)

- [Get started with an existing data source](/docs/grafana-cloud/connect-externally-hosted/existing-datasource/)
- [Private data source connect](/docs/grafana-cloud/connect-externally-hosted/private-data-source-connect/)
- [List of source IPs to add to your allowlist](/docs/grafana-cloud/security-and-account-management/allow-list/)
